---
title: "STAT-615-Group_Project-Model3"
author: "Kevin Chávez-López"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-14"
---

Code for and output a linear regression model that will predict a
quantitative/continuous variable using only one quantitative/continuous
predictor variable. Name this model modelthree

Run the R function summary on modelthree. Point to and discuss the
important metrics for modelthree found in the summary table.

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
```

```{r}
Rats <- read_csv("STAT615_Project1_CLEAN.csv")
Rats
```

```{r}
# Figuring out Intercept and Slope of the equation
  lm(Rats$rt~Rats$iti)->modelthree
  modelthree
  summary(modelthree)
```
Given the really low R-squared (0.0003301) and high (0.432) p-values, we can determine that the duration of a trial is not a factor that varies along with how long it takes for a rat to choose one port vs another. Other factors are more important when it comes to determining the rate of change in Response Times.
